violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the front entrance and back door, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout retail shelving. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of: 1. Retail shelving: pet isle, chip isle (rodent droppings) 2. Milk cooler (brown residue) New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

Raw animal foods (whole shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (hot dogs and sausages) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by small rodent droppings on food items and numerous shelves throughout the store, and on the floor in the store and back area. Rat droppings observed on the cement slab outside the back door, and burrows observed along the fence outside the building. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Ecolab PCS last date of service 3/29/2024. Pest Control Service must be provided daily until no evidence of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024
Food observed stored on shelves and floor with rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024
The dumpster enclosure has an accumulation of trash bags, litter, leaf litter, and debris. Dumpster enclosures must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or rodent droppings and/or debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024

Refuse area and surrounding refuse area was observed with litter and trash. An area for refuse, returnables, and recyclables must be maintained free of litter and clean in order to prevent any harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The floors under retail shelving have an accumulation of soil residue, old rodent droppings and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2023
One bag of cereal and four bags of prunes were observed gnawed. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by gnawed food, gnawed baseboard and rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the retail shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023

The floor underneath retail shelves have an accumulation of soil residue and rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2023
Baseboard was observed gnawed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in food bearing aisles, droppings on towels and gnawed paper towel package. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Contaminated products were voluntarily disposed by PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Small rodent carcass was observed on glue trap under food bearing aisle. Rat carcass was observed in refuse area. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the premise at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023

Inventory was observed stored right against wall in back storage room. Inventory shall be stored at least 12 inches off the wall for pest control to properly assess area for pest activity. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the food shelves and pet food shelves have an accumulation of rodent droppings and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. New Violation. Correct By: 06/15/2023
